title: “It’s public service to be nice to the Professor”
summary: Scott Galloway was invited to the Vanity Fair Oscar Party on Sunday. He spills the tea with Jessica Tarlov, on Raging Moderates.
transcript_html:
YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/shorts/W-u8prvco_8

Transcript

Jessica, I went to the Oscar party at Vanity Fair. The Vanity Fair Oscar party. I don't know if you knew that. >> I did know it, but I want to know more. I want to know everything. You sent me one great text. >> I did. >> I took like five minutes to respond because I want, you know, make you think like, "Oh, she's not just sitting here waiting to hear how the Oscars are." And then nothing. So, tell me everything. >> I'm not exaggerating. I at one point I started googling actors with last name Galloway because I think they screwed up. I don't know. I literally have no idea how I got him. Someone said, "Oh, podcasts are important." And some

19-year-old intern went, "What about that crazy professor?" It was incredible. Standing in between, you know, Jacob Elo and John Ham ordering drinks. >> Did you talk to them? >> I did. I talked to a bunch of people. Everyone was very friendly. At an event like that, you stand out because they're all famous and super successful, so they think it's like public service to be nice to the professor. >> So, he's lonely and pathetic. He'll probably want to speak to me. No, I don't think that's it. I think you sell yourself short. These people know who you are. They at least love what you're doing for young men. I'm sure a

lot of them are pivot listeners. You've seen that. I mean, you guys have become, you know, >> Anyways, I didn't want to go there. I really wasn't fishing for your compliments. It was amazing.
